title: Activated fibroblasts modify keratinocyte stem niche through TET1 and IL-6 to promote their rapid transformation in a mouse model of prenatal arsenic exposure.
summary: Study investigating how prenatal arsenic exposure activates dermal fibroblasts to modify the keratinocyte stem cell niche, promoting skin tumorigenesis in a mouse model.
key_findings: Prenatal arsenic exposure increases activation markers (α-SMA, Fibronectin, Collagen IV) in dermal fibroblasts.
As-conditioned fibroblasts induce TET1 and secrete high levels of IL-6.
IL-6 promotes EMT, proliferation, and migration in keratinocyte stem cells via JAK2-STAT3 signaling.
Inhibition of pSTAT3 reduces EMT and KSC proliferation.
data_availability_statement: The datasets generated and analyzed during the current study are submitted to CSIR-IITR repository and will be made available by corresponding author on request.
